hero.description: Your idea deserves more than a prototype. Your prototype deserves a real foundation.

about.bio1: If you've shipped something real with Cursor or Claude Code and now need someone to take it further, that's the work I'm built for. If you've got a working prototype and a clear picture of where it should go, I've been doing that work for ten years. Both are good places to start from.
about.bio2: I've spent the last <highlight>10+ years</highlight> building things end-to-end: mobile, backend, frontend, infrastructure. The last three on a live event platform used by thousands of people during international tournaments. Cursor, Claude Code, and Copilot are part of my daily workflow, which is why I know where they stop being enough. I don't hand off when my "part" is done. I care about the whole system working, and about keeping it simple enough that someone can pick it up and maintain it when that person is no longer me.
about.bio3: The stack matters less to me than the quality of the product. I want to understand the product, the users, and the direction, not just the ticket on my plate. Most engagements I start become long-term because I stay invested in what I build. If you're looking for a technical partner for the arc of a business rather than a vendor for the next quarter, that's where I'm at my best.

positions.title: Senior Full-Stack Engineer / Tech Lead
positions.description: Led architecture and full-stack development of web (Next.js), mobile (React Native), and backend (Node.js, NestJS) products in a high-load environment. Managed deployment infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, and code reviews as tech lead.

positions.title: Senior Full-Stack Engineer / Tech Lead
positions.description: Led architecture and end-to-end delivery of mobile and backend products as tech lead. Set up automated CI/CD to App Store & Google Play. Managed AWS & standalone infrastructure. Conducted code reviews, interviews, and pre-sale estimations.

positions.title: React Native / Node.js Developer
positions.description: Built cross-platform iOS & Android apps with React Native. Developed GraphQL APIs using Apollo Server and Node.js. Maintained MongoDB and PostgreSQL databases. Contributed to unit testing and code review.

positions.title: Front-End Developer
positions.description: Built SPAs with React, Angular, and TypeScript. Created data visualizations using D3/SVG. Developed accessible markup, unit tests (Karma, Jest), and a Chrome extension. Participated in SCRUM, code reviews, and mentored junior teammates.

positions.title: Junior JavaScript Developer
positions.description: Developed geo-information web portals using ArcGIS Server services, JavaScript, Dojo, Bootstrap, and jQuery.

projects.subtitle: Live products I've built from scratch or rescued — at agency scale and solo. Pick one that looks like yours, then let's talk.
projects.letsTalk: See one that fits? Let's talk →

items.quotePreview:
TLDR: Oleksii owns and delivers. Under pressure, at scale, and in the unknown. Vast experience in Mobile, Web, and Backend within FinTech & SaaS.

I've worked with Oleksii for eight years across multiple products and industries, where
items.quoteRest:
he was performing different types of work - rapidly prototyping, designing scalable architectures for multi-layered systems, leading dev teams, and setting up the best tools and dev practices.

If he is on the project, he monitors all the problems, even if they are outside of his zone. He sees them coming, communicates with them, and takes care of them or guides the responsible person. You never had to explain the task twice - he absorbed it, asked sharp questions, and moved on.

Within our company, he grew from a pure dev role to a lead role. This growth was fueled by his initiatives, continuous personal development, knowledge gathering, and a commitment to establishing a quality-oriented development culture.

I can definitely recommend Oleksii as a results-oriented, experienced software developer/lead who consistently seeks optimal solutions and takes ownership at the project/company levels.

engagement.footerText: Not sure which fits? Start with a discovery call — we'll figure it out together.

cards.title: MVP & Fast Builds
cards.kicker: For founders shipping the first real version, fast
cards.body: This website was built in days using AI-assisted development. That's the pace I bring to your project. Live in 4 to 6 weeks, fixed scope, fixed price. No surprise invoices, no expanding timelines, no rotating team members you've never met.
cards.bestFor: Founders who need to ship the first real version of their product fast, without committing to a full build before they've seen it work.
cards.note: Fast scope. Fixed price. Shipped in weeks.

cards.title: Full Build & Support
cards.kicker: For prototypes that proved the idea and now need to run as a real business
cards.body: Solid architecture from day one, built to scale. I'm embedded in your product long-term: designing, building, leading, and evolving it as your business grows. No handoffs, no agency revolving door, no context lost between quarters. On my last engagement, I shipped features in year three of a product I'd architected in month one. If you grow a team later, I help you hire the right people, or stay on as the tech lead.
cards.bestFor: Founders building something they intend to run for years, who want one technical partner for the whole arc, not a series of handoffs.
cards.note: One partner. Full ownership. Zero handoffs.

cards.kicker: For vibe-coded MVPs that outgrew themselves
cards.title: Vibe-code Rescue
cards.body: You built an MVP with Cursor, Claude Code, or ChatGPT and it proved the idea. But the prototype that gets you to traction isn't the product that scales with you. The codebase gets tangled, features start interfering with each other, and the AI that built it fast can't reason about it anymore. I use the same tools every day, so I know exactly where they run out of road. I keep what works, rebuild what can't, and put real architecture underneath what you've already shipped.
cards.bestFor: Founders whose AI-built MVP validated the idea and now needs real engineering underneath it. Before every new feature becomes a risk.
cards.note: Keep what works. Rebuild what can't. No rewrite.

fit.quote: I do my best work with founders who have a clear picture of the product they want, and want a technical partner to figure out how to build it.
fit.featuredBody: You shipped an MVP with Cursor, Claude Code, or ChatGPT, and it's starting to buckle. I use the same tools every day. I know exactly where they give up, and what to rebuild underneath.
goodFit: You need to ship the first real version of your product in weeks, not over six months with an agency.
You want one person owning your product end to end: mobile, web, backend, and infrastructure.
You're non-technical. You want a partner who makes calls you can trust, not a vendor waiting for instructions.
notFit: I'm at my best as a technical partner who challenges decisions, not as execution-only hands.
I'm deep in the TypeScript/Node world. If your backend is Python, Ruby, Java, or .NET, you'd want someone who lives in that stack.
I lead small teams. If you're staffing a 10-engineer org from day one, you need a hiring CTO — not me.
fit.closingLine: Not sure which you are? That's what the discovery call is for.

contact.heading: Bring me your prototype. I'll tell you what to <gradient>keep</gradient>, what to rebuild, what to ship next.
contact.subheading: Whether you've got a prototype that's working, need a technical partner, or just want to say hi, my inbox is always open.

cookieConsent.title: A note about cookies
cookieConsent.description: I'd like to set one analytics cookie to see which work resonates. No ads, no cross-site tracking. The theme preference is stored separately as an essential cookie. Read more in the <policyLink>privacy page</policyLink>.

hero.badge: Limited spots per cohort · 2 tracks available
hero.subhead: Learn by building a production-grade product — not toy demos. You leave with working software, a GitHub repo recruiters can actually inspect, and the full-stack mindset for the AI era.

stack.body: A modern, production-proven stack used at the companies you want to work for. Every tool is chosen because it's in demand, not because it's trendy.

tracks.body: Both tracks deliver a real, deployable product. The difference is scope and duration. Same mentor, same standards, same outcome — more stack in the extended track.
tracks.scarcityText: Small group — limited spots per cohort.
tracks.scarcitySubtext: Not sure which track fits? Let's figure it out together.

fastTrack.name: Fast Track
fastTrack.tagline: Core stack, real product, career-ready in a quarter.
deliverables: Fully functioning platform (backend + web) on a topic you choose — use it as your MVP
GitHub repo a recruiter can actually read — real architecture, real commits
You're prepared for the AI era — tools, workflows, mindset

deepDive.name: Deep Dive
deepDive.tagline: Full product — backend, web, and mobile app. The complete picture.
deliverables: Complete platform — backend, web app + mobile app (iOS & Android)
GitHub repo a recruiter can actually read — real architecture, real commits
You're prepared for the AI era — tools, AI agents, workflows, mindset
Highload architecture & deployment — your product can scale from day one

fit.quote: I work best with people who are committed to shipping — not just learning about shipping.
goodFit: Junior devs who can write basic code but have never shipped to production
Career changers who completed a bootcamp and want a real project to show
Self-taught devs who want structured guidance and production standards
Anyone who wants a full-stack portfolio piece that's actually deployable
notFit: Complete beginners — you need HTML/CSS/JS basics before this makes sense
Looking for passive video courses to "complete" without building
Expecting theoretical lectures rather than hands-on building sessions
Not ready to commit consistent time each week to actually building

apply.body: Start with a free 30-min call. We'll talk about what you want to build, which track fits, and whether we're a good match.
